skill.name: Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter (.NET)
skill.description: Azure API Center SDK for .NET. Centralized API inventory management with governance, versioning, and discovery.
skill.summary: Manage API inventory and governance using Azure API Center SDK for .NET with Azure CLI.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Documentation-only skill containing Azure API Center SDK usage examples. Static analysis found no executable code patterns. The SKILL.md file provides legitimate Azure SDK documentation for API governance workflows. Environment variables are standard Azure authentication patterns (AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID, etc.) and pose no security risk.

content.user_title: Manage Azure API Center with .NET SDK
content.value_statement: Organize and govern APIs across your enterprise using Azure API Center. This skill provides .NET SDK patterns for creating API inventories, managing versions, and deploying APIs to environments.
actual_capabilities: Create and configure API Center services in Azure subscriptions
Organize APIs into workspaces with logical groupings
Manage API versions and lifecycle stages (design to retired)
Define custom metadata schemas for API governance
Create deployments linking APIs to runtime environments
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and appropriate RBAC permissions
Only supports Azure API Center service (not Azure API Management gateway)
Code examples target .NET SDK only (no Python or TypeScript)
Does not configure API gateway policies or authentication

use_cases.title: Enterprise API Catalog
use_cases.description: Build a centralized inventory of all APIs in your organization with metadata, ownership, and lifecycle tracking.

use_cases.title: API Governance Automation
use_cases.description: Enforce metadata requirements and lifecycle policies across API portfolios using custom schemas.

use_cases.title: API Discovery Portal
use_cases.description: Enable developers to discover available APIs with documentation, versions, and deployment endpoints.
use_cases.target_user: Developer experience teams building internal portals

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Create API Center Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create an Azure API Center service in my subscription. I need to set up API inventory management for my organization. My subscription ID is <subscription-id> and I want to deploy to East US region.

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Register an API
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an OpenAPI specification for our Orders API. Show me how to create a workspace, register the API with metadata, add version v1.0.0, and import the specification.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: API Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a deployment that links our Orders API v1.0.0 to the production environment. The runtime endpoint is https://api.example.com/orders. Include proper error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Metadata Schema Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a JSON schema for API metadata that requires team ownership, cost center, and data classification. Apply it to all APIs in the workspace as mandatory.

output_examples.input: Create an API Center service named 'contoso-apis' in resource group 'platform-rg' with managed identity enabled.
output_examples.output: API Center service 'contoso-apis' created successfully in East US region. System-assigned managed identity enabled with principal ID: abc123... Use this identity to grant Key Vault access for secret management.

output_examples.input: List all APIs in the engineering workspace and their current lifecycle stages.
output_examples.output: Found 5 APIs in workspace 'engineering': Orders API (Production), Payments API (Preview), Inventory API (Production), Auth API (Deprecated), Notifications API (Development).

output_examples.input: Export the OpenAPI specification for Orders API v1.0.0.
output_examples.output: Successfully exported OpenAPI 3.0.1 specification for Orders API v1.0.0. The specification contains 24 endpoints across 4 resource paths: /orders, /orders/{id}, /orders/{id}/items, /orders/{id}/status.

best_practices: Organize APIs by team or domain using workspaces for clear ownership boundaries
Define metadata schemas early and enforce required fields like team and cost center
Update API lifecycle stages as APIs progress from design to production to deprecated
anti_patterns: Creating all APIs in a single workspace without logical grouping
Skipping metadata requirements leading to incomplete API documentation
Leaving deprecated APIs in production stage causing consumer confusion

faq.question: What is the difference between Azure API Center and Azure API Management?
faq.answer: API Center provides centralized API inventory and governance. API Management provides API gateway, policies, and runtime. Use both together: API Center for catalog, API Management for gateway.

faq.question: Do I need an Azure subscription to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need an active Azure subscription with permissions to create ApiCenter resources. Use DefaultAzureCredential with appropriate RBAC roles.

faq.question: Can I import OpenAPI specifications from URLs?
faq.answer: The SDK examples show inline import. For URL imports, download the specification first then pass the content to ImportSpecificationAsync.

faq.question: How do I grant my team access to manage APIs?
faq.answer: Assign Azure RBAC roles at the API Center service or workspace level. Built-in roles include 'API Center Service Contributor' and custom roles for specific operations.

faq.question: What API specification formats are supported?
faq.answer: OpenAPI 2.0 and 3.x, GraphQL SDL, gRPC Protobuf, WSDL for SOAP, and WebSocket schemas. Specify the format when importing.

faq.question: Can I automate API Center operations with CI/CD?
faq.answer: Yes. Use Azure CLI or .NET SDK in GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps pipelines. Authenticate using service principals or managed identities.
